api/README.html.summary: API Documentation Directory This directory is auto-generated by DocFX during the build process. How It Works When you run docfx docfx_project/docfx.json from the repository root, DocFX will: Scan the C# projects specified in docfx.json (configured to look for src/**/*.csproj) Extract XML documentation comments from your code Generate API reference documentation in this directory Create a toc.yml file that organizes the API documentation Important Notes Do not manually edit generated DocFX output files in this folder (such as *.yml and toc.yml) — they will be overwritten each time you run the DocFX build Hand-authored files like index.md and this README.md are intentionally maintained by hand and will be preserved across DocFX runs The actual API reference metadata files (*.yml files) will be generated automatically Template Placeholders The index.md file uses the following template placeholder: Wolfgang.AuditTrail - Will be replaced with your project name

api/Wolfgang.AuditTrail.AuditOperation.h.title: Enum AuditOperation | Wolfgang.AuditTrail Documentation
api/Wolfgang.AuditTrail.AuditOperation.h.summary: Enum AuditOperation Namespace Wolfgang.AuditTrail Assembly Wolfgang.AuditTrail.Abstractions.dll Identifies the type of change captured on an AuditHeader row. The underlying byte values are the ASCII codes for 'I', 'U', and 'D'; the EF model converts the enum through char on the way out so the database column literally contains 'I' / 'U' / 'D' when queried directly. public enum AuditOperation : byte Fields Delete = 68 An entity was deleted. Insert = 73 An entity was inserted. Update = 85 An entity was updated.

api/Wolfgang.AuditTrail.AuditOptions.htm.title: Class AuditOptions | Wolfgang.AuditTrail Documentation
api/Wolfgang.AuditTrail.AuditOptions.htm.summary: Class AuditOptions Namespace Wolfgang.AuditTrail Assembly Wolfgang.AuditTrail.Abstractions.dll Configures how the audit interceptor captures changes and how the schema installer creates the audit tables. public sealed class AuditOptions Inheritance object AuditOptions Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Properties CaptureDeletedValues When true, Delete operations write detail rows containing the pre-delete column values. When false (the default), Delete operations write only a header row. public bool CaptureDeletedValues { get; set; } Property Value bool Remarks Important — stub-attached deletes: the captured values come from PropertyEntry.OriginalValue, which is meaningful only when the entity was actually loaded (or had its originals seeded) before deletion. The classic "stub delete" pattern — context.Set<Customer>().Remove(new Customer { Id = 5 }) — attaches an entity whose non-key properties are CLR defaults, so the detail rows would record those defaults rather than the actual row state. When this flag is on, consumers should load the row first (or call entry.Reload()) before removing it. The captured values are fundamentally a snapshot of what EF Core knew at delete time, not a database read-back. DetailTableName Name of the detail table. Defaults to AuditDetail. public string DetailTableName { get; set; } Property Value string EntityKeySerializer The entity-key serializer used to render primary-key values into the EntityKey column on AuditHeader. Defaults to PipeDelimitedEntityKeySerializer (set by the registration extension; may be null if accessed before registration). public IAuditEntityKeySerializer? EntityKeySerializer { get; set; } Property Value IAuditEntityKeySerializer HeaderTableName Name of the header table. Defaults to AuditHeader. public string HeaderTableName { get; set; } Property Value string Schema Database schema for the AuditHeader and AuditDetail tables. null (the default) uses the provider's default schema: dbo on SQL Server, public on PostgreSQL, none on SQLite/MySQL. public string? Schema { get; set; } Property Value string ValueSerializer The value serializer used by the interceptor to encode column values into detail rows, and (via the schema installer) to drive the detail table's column shape. Defaults to StringAuditValueSerializer (set by the registration extension; may be null if accessed before registration). public IAuditValueSerializer? ValueSerializer { get; set; } Property Value IAuditValueSerializer

api/Wolfgang.AuditTrail.AuditSaveChanges.title: Class AuditSaveChangesInterceptor | Wolfgang.AuditTrail Documentation
api/Wolfgang.AuditTrail.AuditSaveChanges.summary: Class AuditSaveChangesInterceptor Namespace Wolfgang.AuditTrail Assembly Wolfgang.AuditTrail.EntityFrameworkCore.dll Auto-transaction ISaveChangesInterceptor that captures Insert / Update / Delete operations via ChangeTracker and writes audit header + detail rows in the same transaction as the user's save. public sealed class AuditSaveChangesInterceptor : ISaveChangesInterceptor, IInterceptor Inheritance object AuditSaveChangesInterceptor Implements ISaveChangesInterceptor IInterceptor Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Use this when your DbContext already inherits from a third-party base (such as IdentityDbContext<TUser>, a multi-tenant base, or an internal enterprise base) and cannot also inherit from AuditingDbContext. Register via: services.AddEfCoreAuditing<MyUserProvider>(); services.AddDbContext<AppDbContext>((sp, opts) => opts .UseSqlServer(connStr) .UseAuditing(sp)); Cancellation. If the CancellationToken is signalled while SavingChangesAsync is opening the audit transaction, the interceptor catches the resulting OperationCanceledException, disposes the owned transaction, and re-raises — no leak. Cancellation that fires later (during EF's own SQL execution between SavingChangesAsync and SavedChangesAsync) is handled via SaveChangesFailedAsync on EF Core 6 / pre-7-cancel-callback versions; on EF Core 7+ that routes through SaveChangesCanceledAsync instead, which this interceptor doesn't yet hook because that method isn't on the EF Core 6 ISaveChangesInterceptor surface. In that narrow window the owned transaction is disposed when the DbContext is. Inherit from AuditingDbContext for full cancellation robustness — it owns the SaveChangesAsync override and can clean up on every exit path. acceptAllChangesOnSuccess caveat. The interceptor does not support SaveChanges(acceptAllChangesOnSuccess: false): when the caller passes false, EF leaves user entries in their dirty state and the interceptor's audit-pass save would re-emit them. The interceptor detects this case at runtime and throws InvalidOperationException pointing at AuditingDbContext, which threads the value through both passes correctly. Retry-strategy caveat. EF Core's retrying execution strategies (e.g. SqlServerRetryingExecutionStrategy from EnableRetryOnFailure) refuse user-initiated transactions opened outside a strategy delegate. Important: strategy.ExecuteAsync(...) by itself does NOT open a transaction — Database.CurrentTransaction remains null inside it, and this interceptor would still try to open its own owned transaction (then throw via EnsureNonRetryingStrategy). The supported integration is either: Call strategy.ExecuteInTransactionAsync(...) so the strategy opens a transaction the interceptor can enlist in, OR Inside your ExecuteAsync delegate, call context.Database.BeginTransactionAsync(...) yourself before SaveChangesAsync, OR Inherit from AuditingDbContext instead — it composes with retrying strategies correctly out of the box. Rollback / ChangeTracker divergence. If the audit-pass SaveChanges throws after the user-pass SaveChanges has already accepted the user entries (entries are now Unchanged), rolling back the owned transaction puts the database back to the pre-save state but the in-memory ChangeTracker still reflects the accepted state. The interceptor calls Clear() on the rollback path so a stale-state reuse fails loudly the next save — but the safer pattern is to dispose the DbContext after a save failure and create a new one. Constructors AuditSaveChangesInterceptor(IAuditUserProvider, AuditOptions) Initializes a new AuditSaveChangesInterceptor. public AuditSaveChangesInterceptor(IAuditUserProvider userProvider, AuditOptions options) Parameters userProvider IAuditUserProvider options AuditOptions Methods SaveChangesFailed(DbContextErrorEventData) Called when an exception has been thrown in . public void SaveChangesFailed(DbContextErrorEventData eventData) Parameters eventData DbContextErrorEventData Contextual information about the failure. SaveChangesFailedAsync(DbContextErrorEventData, CancellationToken) Called when an exception has been thrown in . public Task SaveChangesFailedAsync(DbContextErrorEventData eventData, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters eventData DbContextErrorEventData Contextual information about the failure. cancellationToken CancellationToken A CancellationToken to observe while waiting for the task to complete. Returns Task A Task representing the asynchronous operation. Exceptions OperationCanceledException If the CancellationToken is canceled. SavedChanges(SaveChangesCompletedEventData, int) Called at the end of . This method is still called if an interceptor suppressed creation of a command in SavingChanges(DbContextEventData, InterceptionResult<int>). In this case, result is the result returned by SavingChanges(DbContextEventData, InterceptionResult<int>). public int SavedChanges(SaveChangesCompletedEventData eventData, int result) Parameters eventData SaveChangesCompletedEventData Contextual information about the DbContext being used. result int The result of the call to . This value is typically used as the return value for the implementation of this method. Returns int The result that EF will use. A normal implementation of this method for any interceptor that is not attempting to change the result is to return the result value passed in. SavedChangesAsync(SaveChangesCompletedEventData, int, CancellationToken) Called at the end of . This method is still called if an interceptor suppressed creation of a command in SavingChangesAsync(DbContextEventData, InterceptionResult<int>, CancellationToken). In this case, result is the result returned by SavingChangesAsync(DbContextEventData, InterceptionResult<int>, CancellationToken). public ValueTask<int> SavedChangesAsync(SaveChangesCompletedEventData eventData, int result, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters eventData SaveChangesCompletedEventData Contextual information about the DbContext being used. result int The result of the call to . This value is typically used as the return value for the implementation of this method. cancellationToken CancellationToken A CancellationToken to observe while waiting for the task to complete. Returns ValueTask<int> The result that EF will use. A normal implementation of this method for any interceptor that is not attempting to change the result is to return the result value passed in. Exceptions OperationCanceledException If the CancellationToken is canceled. SavingChanges(DbContextEventData, InterceptionResult<int>) Called at the start of . public InterceptionResult<int> SavingChanges(DbContextEventData eventData, InterceptionResult<int> result) Parameters eventData DbContextEventData Contextual information about the DbContext being used. result InterceptionResult<int> Represents the current result if one exists. This value will have HasResult set to true if some previous interceptor suppressed execution by calling SuppressWithResult(TResult). This value is typically used as the return value for the implementation of this method. Returns InterceptionResult<int> If HasResult is false, the EF will continue as normal. If HasResult is true, then EF will suppress the operation it was about to perform and use Result instead. A normal implementation of this method for any interceptor that is not attempting to change the result is to return the result value passed in. SavingChangesAsync(DbContextEventData, InterceptionResult<int>, CancellationToken) Called at the start of . public ValueTask<InterceptionResult<int>> SavingChangesAsync(DbContextEventData eventData, InterceptionResult<int> result, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters eventData DbContextEventData Contextual information about the DbContext being used. result InterceptionResult<int> Represents the current result if one exists. This value will have HasResult set to true if some previous interceptor suppressed execution by calling SuppressWithResult(TResult). This value is typically used as the return value for the implementation of this method. cancellationToken CancellationToken A CancellationToken to observe while waiting for the task to complete. Returns ValueTask<InterceptionResult<int>> If HasResult is false, the EF will continue as normal. If HasResult is true, then EF will suppress the operation it was about to perform and use Result instead. A normal implementation of this method for any interceptor that is not attempting to change the result is to return the result value passed in. Exceptions OperationCanceledException If the CancellationToken is canceled.

api/Wolfgang.AuditTrail.AuditUser.html.summary: Struct AuditUser Namespace Wolfgang.AuditTrail Assembly Wolfgang.AuditTrail.Abstractions.dll Identifies the user responsible for a unit of audited work. public readonly record struct AuditUser : IEquatable<AuditUser> Implements IEquatable<AuditUser> Inherited Members ValueType.Equals(object) ValueType.GetHashCode() ValueType.ToString() object.Equals(object, object) object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Remarks UserId is the principal whose credentials authenticated the operation — typically the application's service account or the directly logged-in user. OnBehalfOfUserId is populated in web/service scenarios where the authenticated principal is acting on behalf of a real end user. Example: an ASP.NET Core application runs as svc-orders (the service account => UserId) but handles a request from steve (the human => OnBehalfOfUserId). Constructors AuditUser(string, string?) api/Wolfgang.AuditTrail.AuditingDbContex.title: Class AuditingDbContext | Wolfgang.AuditTrail Documentation
api/Wolfgang.AuditTrail.AuditingDbContex.summary: Class AuditingDbContext Namespace Wolfgang.AuditTrail Assembly Wolfgang.AuditTrail.EntityFrameworkCore.dll Base DbContext that writes audit rows atomically with every SaveChanges() / SaveChangesAsync(CancellationToken) call. Derive your application's context from this instead of DbContext and continue calling SaveChangesAsync as usual — audit capture happens transparently in the same transaction. public abstract class AuditingDbContext : DbContext, IInfrastructure<IServiceProvider>, IDbContextDependencies, IDbSetCache, IDbContextPoolable, IResettableService, IDisposable, IAsyncDisposable Inheritance object DbContext AuditingDbContext Implements IInfrastructure<IServiceProvider> IDbContextDependencies IDbSetCache IDbContextPoolable IResettableService IDisposable IAsyncDisposable Inherited Members DbContext.Set<TEntity>() DbContext.Set<TEntity>(string) DbContext.OnConfiguring(DbContextOptionsBuilder) DbContext.ConfigureConventions(ModelConfigurationBuilder) DbContext.SaveChanges() DbContext.SaveChangesAsync(CancellationToken) DbContext.Dispose() DbContext.DisposeAsync() DbContext.Entry<TEntity>(TEntity) DbContext.Entry(object) DbContext.Add<TEntity>(TEntity) DbContext.AddAsync<TEntity>(TEntity, CancellationToken) DbContext.Attach<TEntity>(TEntity) DbContext.Update<TEntity>(TEntity) DbContext.Remove<TEntity>(TEntity) DbContext.Add(object) DbContext.AddAsync(object, CancellationToken) DbContext.Attach(object) DbContext.Update(object) DbContext.Remove(object) DbContext.AddRange(params object[]) DbContext.AddRangeAsync(params object[]) DbContext.AttachRange(params object[]) DbContext.UpdateRange(params object[]) DbContext.RemoveRange(params object[]) DbContext.AddRange(IEnumerable<object>) DbContext.AddRangeAsync(IEnumerable<object>, CancellationToken) DbContext.AttachRange(IEnumerable<object>) DbContext.UpdateRange(IEnumerable<object>) DbContext.RemoveRange(IEnumerable<object>) DbContext.Find(Type, params object[]) DbContext.FindAsync(Type, params object[]) DbContext.FindAsync(Type, object[], CancellationToken) DbContext.Find<TEntity>(params object[]) DbContext.FindAsync<TEntity>(params object[]) DbContext.FindAsync<TEntity>(object[], CancellationToken) DbContext.FromExpression<TResult>(Expression<Func<IQueryable<TResult>>>) DbContext.Database DbContext.ChangeTracker DbContext.Model DbContext.ContextId DbContext.SavingChanges DbContext.SavedChanges DbContext.SaveChangesFailed object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks This is the recommended integration model for new applications and any context that doesn't already derive from a non-DbContext base. The override wraps the save in IExecutionStrategy's ExecuteInTransactionAsync, so it composes correctly with EnableRetryOnFailure on cloud providers. For applications that already inherit from IdentityDbContext<TUser> or another third-party base, use the auto-transaction interceptor instead — register it via services.AddEfCoreAuditing<TUserProvider>() and options.UseAuditing(serviceProvider). Constructors AuditingDbContext(DbContextOptions, IAuditUserProvider, AuditOptions) Initializes a new AuditingDbContext. protected AuditingDbContext(DbContextOptions options, IAuditUserProvider userProvider, AuditOptions auditOptions) Parameters options DbContextOptions EF Core DbContextOptions. userProvider IAuditUserProvider Supplies the AuditUser stamped on every header. auditOptions AuditOptions Audit configuration including the value / entity-key serializers. Exceptions ArgumentNullException If any argument is null. Properties AuditOptions Audit configuration applied to this context. Exposed so the schema migrator can read schema and table-name overrides without going through the model. public AuditOptions AuditOptions { get; } Property Value AuditOptions Methods OnModelCreating(ModelBuilder) Applies the audit entity mappings. Derived classes that override OnModelCreating must call base.OnModelCreating(modelBuilder) so the audit tables are configured. protected override void OnModelCreating(ModelBuilder modelBuilder) Parameters modelBuilder ModelBuilder SaveChanges(bool) Saves all changes made in this context to the database. This method will automatically call DetectChanges() to discover any changes to entity instances before saving to the underlying database. This can be disabled via AutoDetectChangesEnabled. Entity Framework Core does not support multiple parallel operations being run on the same DbContext instance. This includes both parallel execution of async queries and any explicit concurrent use from multiple threads. Therefore, always await async calls immediately, or use separate DbContext instances for operations that execute in parallel. See Avoiding DbContext threading issues for more information. public override int SaveChanges(bool acceptAllChangesOnSuccess) Parameters acceptAllChangesOnSuccess bool Indicates whether AcceptAllChanges() is called after the changes have been sent successfully to the database. Returns int The number of state entries written to the database. Remarks See Saving data in EF Core for more information. Exceptions DbUpdateException An error is encountered while saving to the database. DbUpdateConcurrencyException A concurrency violation is encountered while saving to the database. A concurrency violation occurs when an unexpected number of rows are affected during save. This is usually because the data in the database has been modified since it was loaded into memory. SaveChangesAsync(bool, CancellationToken) Saves all changes made in this context to the database. This method will automatically call DetectChanges() to discover any changes to entity instances before saving to the underlying database. This can be disabled via AutoDetectChangesEnabled. Entity Framework Core does not support multiple parallel operations being run on the same DbContext instance. This includes both parallel execution of async queries and any explicit concurrent use from multiple threads. Therefore, always await async calls immediately, or use separate DbContext instances for operations that execute in parallel. See Avoiding DbContext threading issues for more information. public override Task<int> SaveChangesAsync(bool acceptAllChangesOnSuccess, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters acceptAllChangesOnSuccess bool Indicates whether AcceptAllChanges() is called after the changes have been sent successfully to the database. cancellationToken CancellationToken A CancellationToken to observe while waiting for the task to complete. Returns Task<int> A task that represents the asynchronous save operation. The task result contains the number of state entries written to the database. Remarks See Saving data in EF Core for more information. Exceptions DbUpdateException An error is encountered while saving to the database. DbUpdateConcurrencyException A concurrency violation is encountered while saving to the database. A concurrency violation occurs when an unexpected number of rows are affected during save. This is usually because the data in the database has been modified since it was loaded into memory. OperationCanceledException If the CancellationToken is canceled.

api/Wolfgang.AuditTrail.DbContextOptions.title: Class DbContextOptionsBuilderExtensions | Wolfgang.AuditTrail Documentation
api/Wolfgang.AuditTrail.DbContextOptions.summary: Class DbContextOptionsBuilderExtensions Namespace Wolfgang.AuditTrail Assembly Wolfgang.AuditTrail.EntityFrameworkCore.dll EF Core DbContextOptionsBuilder extensions that wire the AuditSaveChangesInterceptor into a consumer's DbContext. public static class DbContextOptionsBuilderExtensions Inheritance object DbContextOptionsBuilderExtensions Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods UseAuditing(DbContextOptionsBuilder, IServiceProvider) Registers the audit save-changes interceptor on this DbContextOptionsBuilder. Use this for applications whose DbContext already inherits from a third-party base (e.g. IdentityDbContext<TUser>) and therefore cannot also inherit from AuditingDbContext. public static DbContextOptionsBuilder UseAuditing(this DbContextOptionsBuilder optionsBuilder, IServiceProvider serviceProvider) Parameters optionsBuilder DbContextOptionsBuilder The EF Core options builder. serviceProvider IServiceProvider The DI service provider; must already contain AuditOptions and IAuditUserProvider (register them via services.AddEfCoreAuditing<TUserProvider>()). Returns DbContextOptionsBuilder optionsBuilder for chaining. Remarks You must also call modelBuilder.ApplyAuditing(options) from your DbContext.OnModelCreating override. Unlike AuditingDbContext (which applies the audit entity mappings itself in its OnModelCreating), the interceptor cannot reach into the consumer's model — the EF Core model is sealed by the time the interceptor first runs. Without the ApplyAuditing call, the first save with audit data will throw an "entity type was not found in the model" exception when the interceptor tries to Add the AuditHeader / AuditDetail entities. Minimal correct wiring: services.AddEfCoreAuditing<MyUserProvider>(); services.AddDbContext<AppDbContext>((sp, opts) => opts .UseSqlServer(connStr) .UseAuditing(sp)); public class AppDbContext : IdentityDbContext<AppUser> { private readonly AuditOptions _auditOptions; public AppDbContext(DbContextOptions options, AuditOptions auditOptions) : base(options) => _auditOptions = auditOptions; protected override void OnModelCreating(ModelBuilder modelBuilder) { base.OnModelCreating(modelBuilder); modelBuilder.ApplyAuditing(_auditOptions); // <-- required } }

Header { get; set; } Property Value AuditHeader HeaderId Foreign key to the owning AuditHeader. public Guid HeaderId { get; set; } Property Value Guid ValueText Culture-invariant text representation of the captured column value, encoded by the active IAuditValueSerializer. For Insert and Update this is the new (post-change) value; for Delete with AuditOptions.CaptureDeletedValues = true this is the pre-delete (original) value. null when the audited value was itself null. public string? ValueText { get; set; } Property Value string ValueType Discriminator identifying the CLR type of the value: String, Int32, DateTime, Guid, Enum:<FullName>, etc. public string ValueType { get; set; } Property Value string

api/Wolfgang.AuditTrail.Entities.AuditHe.summary: Class AuditHeader Namespace Wolfgang.AuditTrail.Entities Assembly Wolfgang.AuditTrail.Abstractions.dll One row per audited entity change. Grouped with sibling header rows from the same SaveChanges call via TransactionId. public class AuditHeader Inheritance object AuditHeader Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Properties AuditedAtUtc UTC timestamp captured when the interceptor wrote the row. public DateTime AuditedAtUtc { get; set; } Property Value DateTime Details Navigation to the per-column detail rows. public ICollection<AuditDetail> Details { get; set; } Property Value ICollection<AuditDetail> EntityKey Primary-key value(s) serialized by the configured IAuditEntityKeySerializer. For single-column keys this is the key's string form; for composite keys it is the serializer's chosen encoding. public string EntityKey { get; set; } Property Value string EntityTable Fully-qualified table name (including schema) the entity maps to. public string EntityTable { get; set; } Property Value string EntityType CLR full type name of the audited entity. public string EntityType { get; set; } Property Value string HeaderId Primary key. public Guid HeaderId { get; set; } Property Value Guid OnBehalfOfUserId End user the authenticated principal was acting on behalf of (web scenarios where a service account handles a request from a human user). null in non-impersonation scenarios. public string? OnBehalfOfUserId { get; set; } Property Value string Operation The type of change. See AuditOperation. public AuditOperation Operation { get; set; } Property Value AuditOperation TransactionId Shared by every header row produced by a single SaveChanges call. Lets consumers reconstruct "what changed together" without joining on timestamps. public Guid TransactionId { get; set; } Property Value Guid UserId Authenticated principal — typically the service account running the app, or the directly logged-in user. public string UserId { get; set; } Property Value string

api/Wolfgang.AuditTrail.IAuditEntityKeyS.title: Interface IAuditEntityKeySerializer | Wolfgang.AuditTrail Documentation
api/Wolfgang.AuditTrail.IAuditEntityKeyS.summary: Interface IAuditEntityKeySerializer Namespace Wolfgang.AuditTrail Assembly Wolfgang.AuditTrail.Abstractions.dll Serializes an entity's primary key value(s) into the string stored in AuditHeader.EntityKey. public interface IAuditEntityKeySerializer Remarks Composite keys are passed as a multi-element sequence; single-column keys arrive as a sequence of one element. The default implementation joins parts with '|'; consumers can swap to JSON or any other format. Methods Serialize(IReadOnlyList<object?>) Renders the given primary-key values as the string persisted in AuditHeader.EntityKey. string Serialize(IReadOnlyList<object?> keyValues) Parameters keyValues IReadOnlyList<object> Returns string

api/Wolfgang.AuditTrail.IAuditUserProvid.summary: Interface IAuditUserProvider Namespace Wolfgang.AuditTrail Assembly Wolfgang.AuditTrail.Abstractions.dll Supplies the AuditUser stamped onto each audit header row. public interface IAuditUserProvider Remarks Consumers implement this and register it with their DI container. The interceptor resolves it once per SaveChanges call and writes the result to every header row produced by that call. Methods GetCurrentUser() Returns the user identity to associate with the currently-in-flight save. AuditUser GetCurrentUser() Returns AuditUser

api/Wolfgang.AuditTrail.IAuditValueSeria.title: Interface IAuditValueSerializer | Wolfgang.AuditTrail Documentation
api/Wolfgang.AuditTrail.IAuditValueSeria.summary: Interface IAuditValueSerializer Namespace Wolfgang.AuditTrail Assembly Wolfgang.AuditTrail.Abstractions.dll Encodes values captured by the audit interceptor into the column(s) it persists on the AuditDetail row, and decodes them back on read. public interface IAuditValueSerializer Remarks The serializer declares the value column(s) it expects on the AuditDetail table via Columns. v1's DDL path is just Database.EnsureCreatedAsync against the EF Core model — which contains the fixed ValueText / ValueBinary property set on AuditDetail regardless of which serializer is configured. Columns is therefore advisory in v1 (used by the serializer to keep its writes consistent with the entity properties); a future schema installer that emits per-serializer DDL is planned but not shipped. v1 ships StringAuditValueSerializer (text-only). Future implementations (binary, hybrid) are additive — they slot in without changing this interface. Properties Columns The columns this serializer expects on the AuditDetail table, in the order they should appear. Advisory in v1 — see remarks on the interface. can construct it directly with a DbContextOptions<TContext> configured for any EF Core provider (SQL Server, PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite). Consumer applications normally never touch this type — they call MigrateAuditSchemaAsync on their own AuditingDbContext instead. Constructors AuditMigrationsDbContext(DbContextOptions<AuditMigrationsDbContext>, AuditOptions) Constructs the migrations context with a provider-configured DbContextOptions<TContext> and the live AuditOptions that drive table/schema naming. public AuditMigrationsDbContext(DbContextOptions<AuditMigrationsDbContext> dbOptions, AuditOptions options) Parameters dbOptions DbContextOptions<AuditMigrationsDbContext> options AuditOptions Properties Options The schema-name / table-name overrides applied when the audit entities are configured on the model. public AuditOptions Options { get; } Property Value AuditOptions Methods OnModelCreating(ModelBuilder) Override this method to further configure the model that was discovered by convention from the entity types exposed in DbSet<TEntity> properties on your derived context. The resulting model may be cached and re-used for subsequent instances of your derived context. protected override void OnModelCreating(ModelBuilder modelBuilder) Parameters modelBuilder ModelBuilder The builder being used to construct the model for this context. Databases (and other extensions) typically define extension methods on this object that allow you to configure aspects of the model that are specific to a given database. Remarks If a model is explicitly set on the options for this context (via UseModel(IModel)) then this method will not be run. See Modeling entity types and relationships for more information.

api/Wolfgang.AuditTrail.Schema.AuditSche.title: Class AuditSchemaInstaller | Wolfgang.AuditTrail Documentation
api/Wolfgang.AuditTrail.Schema.AuditSche.summary: Class AuditSchemaInstaller Namespace Wolfgang.AuditTrail.Schema Assembly Wolfgang.AuditTrail.EntityFrameworkCore.dll Creates and drops the audit tables in the consumer's database. public sealed class AuditSchemaInstaller Inheritance object AuditSchemaInstaller Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks v1 uses EF Core's own model facilities — Database.EnsureCreatedAsync applied to a context that has the audit entities configured will produce the right DDL for any supported provider. For consumers using EF Core Migrations, an alternative is to let migrations own the audit tables; this helper is for the non-migration path (prototypes, self-contained desktop apps, tests). Constructors AuditSchemaInstaller(AuditOptions) Constructs an installer bound to the given options. public AuditSchemaInstaller(AuditOptions options) Parameters options AuditOptions Methods CreateTablesAsync(DbContext, CancellationToken) Ensures the audit header and detail tables exist on the database backing the supplied context. Safe to call repeatedly. [Obsolete("Use AuditingDbContext.MigrateAuditSchemaAsync (or AuditSchemaMigrator.RunAsync) — the new path uses EF Core's model differ + provider SQL generator, supports schema evolution, and creates only the audit tables instead of every table in the context's model.")] public Task CreateTablesAsync(DbContext context, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters context DbContext cancellationToken CancellationToken Returns Task Remarks Important: this delegates to EnsureCreatedAsync(CancellationToken), which creates every table in the supplied context's model — not just the audit tables. That's the right behavior for a dedicated audit context, a fresh in-memory test database, or a self-contained desktop app. For shared contexts that already manage their own schema via EF Core Migrations, do NOT call this — let the consumer's migration pipeline handle the audit tables alongside its own. DropTablesAsync(DbContext, CancellationToken) Drops the audit tables, if present. Intended for tests; use with care in production. public Task DropTablesAsync(DbContext context, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters context DbContext cancellationToken CancellationToken Returns Task Exceptions ArgumentNullException If context is null. ArgumentException If the configured schema or table names are null or whitespace. InvalidOperationException If the configured schema or table names contain characters that would be unsafe to interpolate into raw SQL.

api/Wolfgang.AuditTrail.Schema.AuditSche.summary: Class AuditSchemaVersion Namespace Wolfgang.AuditTrail.Schema Assembly Wolfgang.AuditTrail.EntityFrameworkCore.dll One-row record tracking the audit schema version currently installed in the target database. The row is upserted by AuditSchemaMigrator after a successful migration apply. public sealed class AuditSchemaVersion Inheritance object AuditSchemaVersion Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Properties Id Single-row primary key. Always 1. Modelled this way so EF Core's model differ treats the row as a fixed identity (and so the version table always has exactly one row regardless of how many times we upsert). public int Id { get; set; } Property Value int Version The installed schema version. Increments only when a new AuditSchemaSnapshotV{N} is shipped. public int Version { get; set; } Property Value int

api/Wolfgang.AuditTrail.Serializers.Stri.title: Class StringAuditValueSerializer | Wolfgang.AuditTrail Documentation
api/Wolfgang.AuditTrail.Serializers.Stri.summary: Class StringAuditValueSerializer Namespace Wolfgang.AuditTrail.Serializers Assembly Wolfgang.AuditTrail.EntityFrameworkCore.dll v1 default IAuditValueSerializer. Writes every value as culture-invariant text to the ValueText column on the AuditDetail table. public sealed class StringAuditValueSerializer : IAuditValueSerializer Inheritance object StringAuditValueSerializer Implements IAuditValueSerializer Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Encoding rules: String stored as-is. Integral, decimal, and floating-point types use InvariantCulture. DateTime and DateTimeOffset use the round-trip "o" format. Guid uses the "D" format (32 digits separated by hyphens). bool renders as True / False. byte[] is base64-encoded. Enums use ToString() (the symbolic name); the discriminator records the enum's full type name. null writes SQL NULL; the discriminator still records the declared CLR type. Fields ValueColumnName The name of the single text column this serializer writes. public const string ValueColumnName = "ValueText" Field Value string Properties Columns The columns this serializer expects on the AuditDetail table, in the order they should appear. Advisory in v1 — see remarks on the interface. public IReadOnlyList<AuditValueColumn> Columns { get; } Property Value IReadOnlyList<AuditValueColumn> Methods Decode(IAuditValueReader, string) Decodes a value previously produced by Encode(object?, Type, IAuditValueWriter). public object? Returns object Encode(object?, Type, IAuditValueWriter) Encodes value (of declared CLR type clrType) into writer. public string Encode(object? value, Type clrType, IAuditValueWriter writer) Parameters value object The value to encode. May be null. clrType Type The declared CLR type of the audited property. writer IAuditValueWriter Receives the encoded representation. Returns string The ValueType discriminator to persist alongside the value.

api/Wolfgang.AuditTrail.ServiceCollectio.summary: Class ServiceCollectionExtensions Namespace Wolfgang.AuditTrail Assembly Wolfgang.AuditTrail.EntityFrameworkCore.dll Dependency-injection helpers for registering the audit options and user provider. public static class ServiceCollectionExtensions Inheritance object ServiceCollectionExtensions Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods AddEfCoreAuditing<TUserProvider>(IServiceCollection, Action<AuditOptions>?) Registers AuditOptions (as a singleton) and TUserProvider as IAuditUserProvider (scoped). Pick one of the two integration models: Derive your DbContext from AuditingDbContext (recommended for greenfield contexts), or Call UseAuditing(DbContextOptionsBuilder, IServiceProvider) on the DbContextOptionsBuilder when registering your context (for contexts already inheriting from a third-party base such as IdentityDbContext<TUser>). Either way, consumers continue to call plain SaveChangesAsync(CancellationToken) at every call site — audit rows are written atomically in the same transaction. public static IServiceCollection AddEfCoreAuditing<TUserProvider>(this IServiceCollection services, Action<AuditOptions>? configure = null) where TUserProvider : class, IAuditUserProvider Parameters services IServiceCollection configure Action<AuditOptions> Returns IServiceCollection Type Parameters TUserProvider

api/Wolfgang.AuditTrail.html.title: Namespace Wolfgang.AuditTrail | Wolfgang.AuditTrail Documentation
api/Wolfgang.AuditTrail.html.summary: Namespace Wolfgang.AuditTrail Classes AuditOptions Configures how the audit interceptor captures changes and how the schema installer creates the audit tables. AuditSaveChangesInterceptor Auto-transaction ISaveChangesInterceptor that captures Insert / Update / Delete operations via ChangeTracker and writes audit header + detail rows in the same transaction as the user's save. AuditValueColumn Describes a column that an IAuditValueSerializer requires on the AuditDetail table. AuditingDbContext Base DbContext that writes audit rows atomically with every SaveChanges() / SaveChangesAsync(CancellationToken) call. Derive your application's context from this instead of DbContext and continue calling SaveChangesAsync as usual — audit capture happens transparently in the same transaction. DbContextOptionsBuilderExtensions EF Core DbContextOptionsBuilder extensions that wire the AuditSaveChangesInterceptor into a consumer's DbContext. ModelBuilderExtensions EF Core model-configuration extensions that register the audit entity types on a consumer's DbContext. NotAuditedAttribute Excludes an entity type or a single property from audit capture. ServiceCollectionExtensions Dependency-injection helpers for registering the audit options and user provider. Structs AuditUser Identifies the user responsible for a unit of audited work. Interfaces IAuditEntityKeySerializer Serializes an entity's primary key value(s) into the string stored in AuditHeader.EntityKey. IAuditUserProvider Supplies the AuditUser stamped onto each audit header row. IAuditValueReader Supplies stored column values to an IAuditValueSerializer during decode. IAuditValueSerializer Encodes values captured by the audit interceptor into the column(s) it persists on the AuditDetail row, and decodes them back on read. IAuditValueWriter Receives the serialized form of a value being captured into an AuditDetail row. Enums AuditOperation Identifies the type of change captured on an AuditHeader row. The underlying byte values are the ASCII codes for 'I', 'U', and 'D'; the EF model converts the enum through char on the way out so the database column literally contains 'I' / 'U' / 'D' when queried directly.

docs/getting-started.html.summary: Getting Started This guide will help you quickly get up and running with Wolfgang.AuditTrail.
